ccel.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

source.title: ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix
ANF05. Fathers of the Third Century: Hippolytus, Cyprian, Caius, Novatian, Appendix

verses.text: And (Marcus alleged) that the Quaternion, after having explained these things, spoke as follows: “Now, I wish also to exhibit to you Truth herself, for I have brought her down from the mansions above, in order that you may behold her naked, and become acquainted with her beauty; nay, also that you may hear her speak, and may marvel at her wisdom.

Observe,” says the Quaternion, “then, first, the head above, Alpha (and long) O; the neck, B and P[si]; shoulders, along with hands, G and C[hi]; breasts, Delta and P[hi]; diaphragm, Eu; belly, Z and T; pudenda, Eta and S; thighs, T[h] and R; knees, Ip; calves, Ko; ankles, Lx[si]; feet, M and N.”
This is in the body of Truth, according to Marcus.
This is the figure of the element; this the character of the letter.

And he styles this element Man, and affirms it to be the source of every word, and the originating principle of every sound, and the realization in speech of everything that is ineffable, and a mouth of taciturn silence.
And this is the body of (Truth) herself.
But do you, raising aloft the conceiving power of the understanding, hear from the mouths of Truth (of) the Logos, who is Self-generator and Progenitor.
